file.content:
---
type: outcome
id: oc-fc348f0
status: done
title: Hetzner replication mechanism decided (git mirror vs ZFS send vs restic — or combination)
created: 2026-07-02
blocked_by: []
---

# Hetzner replication mechanism decided

**Actor: patflynn (human).** The options table — (a) git-native mirror, (b) ZFS send, (c) restic/borg, not mutually exclusive — is in [roadmap.md Phase 0](../../design/roadmap.md#phase-0--mvp-repos-off-github). This node is deliberately the experiment's first human-blocked frontier item ([[ida-3145b7a]]): unblocked in the graph, waiting only on the owner's decision.

Done 2026-07-04 — decided as (a) + (c) in [dcr-db1acbb](../decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md).

input.description: Find how decision nodes are referenced elsewhere

content.text: Let me check how cross-references between knowledge nodes are conventionally written in this repo.

input.command: cd /tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260704-1614-8477a6b8/.the-valley && grep -rn '\[\[' --include="*.md" . | head -20; echo "---"; grep -rn 'wiki\|\[\[' README.md | head -10; echo "---"; ls decisions/ 2>/dev/null
input.description: Check wiki-link conventions in knowledge graph

content.content:
outcomes/oc-9949561-push-replication.md:12:The S1 infrastructure work: bare repos on classic-laddie as primary, offsite replication to Hetzner, and one full restore *performed and verified* — configured is not done ([user-scenarios.md § S1](../../design/user-scenarios.md#s1--my-repos-live-on-my-infrastructure-and-i-can-never-lose-them), [roadmap.md Phase 0](../../design/roadmap.md#phase-0--mvp-repos-off-github)). The implementation lives in cosmo; it is tracked here. Blocked by the mechanism decision [[oc-fc348f0]].
outcomes/oc-2fbcd7b-roadmap-rederived.md:12:Once the requirements are rewritten from the [scenario ladder](../../design/user-scenarios.md), [roadmap.md](../../design/roadmap.md)'s phases should be checked against the rungs — phases and rungs ought to correspond, and where they don't, the roadmap moves. Blocked by [[oc-49555c7]].
outcomes/oc-fc348f0-hetzner-mechanism.md:12:**Actor: patflynn (human).** The options table — (a) git-native mirror, (b) ZFS send, (c) restic/borg, not mutually exclusive — is in [roadmap.md Phase 0](../../design/roadmap.md#phase-0--mvp-repos-off-github). This node is deliberately the experiment's first human-blocked frontier item ([[ida-3145b7a]]): unblocked in the graph, waiting only on the owner's decision.
ideas/ida-8482624-federation-groups.md:77:- Disposition of this material: [[dcr-2113c52]] ([decisions/dcr-2113c52-pr1-relanding.md](../decisions/dcr-2113c52-pr1-relanding.md))
ideas/ida-8482624-federation-groups.md:78:- The engine this layer hosts: [[ida-eac723e]] ([ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md](./ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md))
ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md:12:> Extends the adopted outcome-DAG sketch: [[ida-eac723e]] ([ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md](./ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md)); the thin bet is [architecture.md § the knowledge graph read generatively — an outcome DAG](../../design/architecture.md#bet-the-knowledge-graph-read-generatively--an-outcome-dag). Status is *exploring*, not adopted — this node is an experiment candidate, and its first test is already running: the seeded [outcomes/](../outcomes/) DAG rooted at [[oc-f3bcfd0]] ([oc-f3bcfd0-s1-holds.md](../outcomes/oc-f3bcfd0-s1-holds.md)).
ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md:22:Stated plainly, the consequence: the priority-layer question that [[ida-eac723e]] split in two — **work-scheduling** vs **attention-routing** — partially *reunifies*. Attention-routing is work-scheduling for the actor class that cannot be spawned. A human-blocked outcome is not a different kind of thing from an agent-dispatchable one; it is a frontier node whose assigned actor happens to be reached by notification rather than by process creation. (The firehose half of attention-routing — what a human must *see* across all finished work — stays a separate open question; only the "human as blocking producer" half folds back in.)
ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md:51:- The sketch this extends: [[ida-eac723e]] ([ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md](./ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md))
ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md:53:- First test: the live [outcomes/](../outcomes/) DAG rooted at [[oc-f3bcfd0]], with [[oc-fc348f0]] as the first deliberately human-blocked frontier item
ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:90:- Disposition of this material: [[dcr-2113c52]] ([decisions/dcr-2113c52-pr1-relanding.md](../decisions/dcr-2113c52-pr1-relanding.md))
ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md:91:- The organizational layer the scheduler runs inside: [[ida-8482624]] ([ida-8482624-federation-groups.md](./ida-8482624-federation-groups.md))
decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md:17:Closes the mechanism question tracked by [[oc-fc348f0]]. Immediate follow-up: the owner must provision the Hetzner VPS and Storage Box before the implementation outcome [[oc-9949561]] can complete; the implementation itself lives in cosmo.
decisions/dcr-2113c52-pr1-relanding.md:15:- The **full prose is preserved as idea nodes** (this PR): [[ida-eac723e]] (outcome DAG) and [[ida-8482624]] (federation/groups), with the substance the self-transparency stub had dropped appended to [self-transparency.md](../../design/self-transparency.md).
---
---
dcr-2113c52-pr1-relanding.md
dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md

content.text:
All done. PR #9 had one review comment, and it's now addressed, pushed, and replied to.

**What the comment asked:** In `.the-valley/outcomes/oc-fc348f0-hetzner-mechanism.md`, the new "Done" line referenced the decision node with a plain relative Markdown link, while the rest of the knowledge graph uses `[[id]]` wiki-link syntax for node-to-node references.

**What I changed:** I confirmed the convention across the repo — node references consistently use `[[id]]`, frequently paired with a parenthetical relative link for GitHub navigation (e.g. `[[dcr-2113c52]] ([decisions/...](...))` in the idea nodes). So rather than taking the bot's suggestion verbatim (which would have dropped the clickable path), I applied the dual form: `[[dcr-db1acbb]] ([dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md](../decisions/...))`. That's commit `3eee534`, pushed to the PR branch.

There was no build or test suite to run — the repo is Markdown only — but I verified the link target file exists. I replied to the comment explaining the change and why I kept the parenthetical link. The untracked `.claude/` directory is local harness config, so I left it out of the commit.
